content.question: If 6/8-year tracks were abolished without other lower-secondary reforms, how much sorting behavior would displace into private schools, church-school conversions, cross-municipal transfers, or parent-driven tutoring/remediation rather than disappear?
content.reduction_path: Model historical displacement patterns from track-removal in other European systems (e.g., UK comprehensive school transitions, Scandinavian detracking); survey Hungarian parents' contingent preferences (if tracks closed, would you pursue private school, church school, etc.); track enrollment shifts in municipalities or school sectors that have de facto reduced tracking
